data.title: Bitcoin and Strategy Show Bullish Signals with Golden Cross
data.description:
According to Odaily, recent developments in the cryptocurrency market have highlighted a potential bullish trend for Bitcoin (BTC) and its largest publicly traded holding institution, Strategy (MSTR). Both entities have exhibited a 'golden cross' pattern on their daily charts, where the 50-day simple moving average (SMA) surpasses the 100-day SMA. This technical formation suggests that the short-term trend is stronger than the long-term trend, potentially indicating the start of a new upward cycle. Additional technical indicators, such as the MACD, also support this optimistic outlook.

Despite these positive signals, analysts caution that Bitcoin's current price is fluctuating between $101,000 and $107,000. A drop below $100,000 could trigger further profit-taking, with a support level near $98,000. As the market continues to monitor these developments, investors remain attentive to potential shifts in Bitcoin's trajectory.

data.title: Movement Labs was revealed to have secretly promised to provide up to 10% of the token shares to two advisors
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Leaked internal documents of Trump-backed crypto startup Movement Labs show that it had signed a memorandum with two consultants, promising to grant them up to 10% of the total supply of MOVE tokens. These agreements were not disclosed to investors or the public. One of the consultants, who was internally called a "shadow co-founder," is currently seeking legal means to cash out tokens worth more than $50 million. Movement Labs said the agreements were non-binding, but the incident exacerbated the company's previous controversy over the market manipulation scandal and led to public disagreements between the co-founders. (CoinDesk)

data.description: According to Binance Futures data, over the past 24 hours, the ETH/USDT, BTC/USDT, SOL/USDT, NXPC/USDT and XRP/USDT trading pairs led the USD-M perpetual futures market in trading volume.ETH/USDT perpetual futures long/short ratio: 1.94, funding rate: 0.0063%.BTC/USDT perpetual futures long/short ratio: 0.75, funding rate: 0.0022%.SOL/USDT perpetual futures long/short ratio: 2.17, funding rate: 0.0003%.NXPC/USDT perpetual futures long/short ratio: 0.74, funding rate: -0.1840%.XRP/USDT perpetual futures long/short ratio: 2.94, funding rate: -0.0033%.

data.title: A whale/institution withdrew 3261 BTC from CEX in the past 4 hours
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale/institution withdrew 3,261 BTC from Binance and Kraken in the past 4 hours, worth US$333.36 million. The whale currently holds 18,330 BTC worth US$1.87 billion.

data.title: Wintermute opens New York office, citing improved US crypto rules
data.description:
Wintermute, a London-based algorithmic crypto trading and market-making firm, has opened an office in New York as part of its expansion into the US. Wintermute announced the opening of its New York office on May 15, citing improved regulatory conditions in the world’s largest economy. “As the US takes a friendlier stance on digital assets and institutional adoption accelerates, we moved quickly to establish roots in New York City,” the company wrote in a May 15 X post, adding that the local presence will help them in “contributing to the future regulatory framework
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/wintermute-new-york-office-improved-us-crypto-regulations?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: JPMorgan Chase: Bitcoin's upside may exceed gold in the second half of 2025
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: JPMorgan analysts said that although gold still has room to rise in the second half of 2025, Bitcoin may perform more strongly, and its price may be driven up as companies purchase Bitcoin and U.S. states increase their support for cryptocurrencies. In contrast, the rise in gold mainly benefits from the continued purchases by central banks and investors. The average price is expected to be US$3,675 per ounce in the fourth quarter of 2025 and may exceed US$4,000 per ounce in the second quarter of 2026. However, JPMorgan believes that Bitcoin has greater potential for growth, especially in the current macroeconomic environment, as investors' confidence in the US dollar weakens and they seek alternative assets to hedge risks. (The Block)

data.title: Paidun: Zunami was hacked and lost about $500,000
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Paidun monitoring, the yield aggregation protocol Zunami Protocol was hacked, and the collateral of zunUSD and zunETH was stolen, resulting in a loss of about $500,000. The attacker has transferred the stolen funds to TornadoCash. Zunami Protocol was also attacked in August 2023, with a loss of at least $2.1 million.

data.title: $78 Million Lost to ‘Laundering Loophole’ in Tether Freezing Method Since 2017
data.description:
An AMLBot report claims that Tether’s multi-signature set up for freezing funds creates a “critical window” for illicit actors to exploit.
source: https://decrypt.co/320223/78m-lost-laundering-loophole-tether-usdt-freezing

data.title: Telegram Launches Content Creator Competition to Counter WhatsApp
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Telegram founder Pavel Durov announced the launch of the first content creator competition on his personal channel. The aim is to produce a viral video showcasing how Telegram consistently outpaces its imitator, WhatsApp. Durov stated that WhatsApp has initiated a smear campaign against Telegram, prompting the need for a counteraction. He emphasized that the gloves are off and they are ready to engage.

Durov expressed the need to awaken WhatsApp users, who he believes are stuck using outdated versions of features that Telegram pioneered long ago. To assist creators, Telegram has compiled a list of 30 major features that Telegram introduced first, which were later replicated by WhatsApp. This list is not exhaustive and does not cover many features exclusive to Telegram users, which WhatsApp still lacks. Durov confidently asserted that Telegram remains far ahead of WhatsApp in terms of innovation and functionality.

data.title: Delay in processing Tether blacklist resulted in $78 million USDT not being frozen in time
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Blockchain compliance company AMLBot released a report stating that Tether had a delay in blacklisting suspected addresses, resulting in more than $78 million in USDT being transferred before the freeze took effect. The delay stems from Tether's multi-signature contract mechanism on Ethereum and Tron. The blacklist process requires two multi-signature transaction confirmations, with an average time difference of 44 minutes. During this window period, attackers can monitor blacklist submission transactions in real time and quickly transfer assets to circumvent freezing measures. The report pointed out that since 2017, about 170 Tron addresses have taken advantage of this delay, with an average of nearly $290,000 per address transferred before being officially blacklisted. The incident has raised questions about the efficiency of Tether's compliance enforcement. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Telegram launches content creator competition to challenge WhatsApp
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Telegram founder and CEO Durov posted that the platform has launched its first content creator competition, encouraging users to make videos to reveal how Telegram has long been ahead of the "cheap imitator" WhatsApp. He accused WhatsApp of launching a smear campaign and said he would no longer "repay evil with kindness." The event prize is $50,000 and the deadline is 23:59 on May 26 (Dubai time). Participants need to make a short video in English within 180 seconds to show the fact that Telegram is ahead of WhatsApp in multiple functions. The selection criteria include clarity, visual impact, viral potential and meme value. The results will be announced in June 2025.

data.title: Virtual Currency's Role in West Balkan Crime Networks Intensifies
data.description: According to PANews, a recent study by the Global Initiative Against Transnational Organized Crime (GI-TOC) reveals that virtual currencies are increasingly significant in criminal activities in the West Balkan region. Criminal networks in the area have transferred tens of millions of euros through cryptocurrency wallets. While the legal use of cryptocurrencies is on the rise, illegal activities involving them are also rapidly increasing, particularly in countries like Albania and Serbia. So far, only three cases of cryptocurrency asset seizures have been reported in the region. GI-TOC analysts highlight that local authorities face challenges in regulation, technical expertise, and cross-border cooperation, making the region vulnerable in combating illegal virtual currency activities.

data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News MetaMask co-founder confirmed that it plans to support native SOL this month. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: YZi Labs launches global incubation project EASY Residency and appoints Cathy Dou as head
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the announcement of YZi Labs, the company announced the appointment of Cathy Dou as the head of incubation operations, responsible for the 10-week offline incubation project EASY Residency launched on June 2. The first stop of the project is located in Silicon Valley, California, and it is aimed at top startup teams in the fields of Web3, AI and healthcare, providing funding, accommodation, meals and mentor resource support. Subsequent plans are to expand to Singapore, Dubai and other places. Applications will be closed at 14:59 on May 22, Beijing time.

data.title: Fed Chairman Powell "admits his mistake": the era of long-term low interest rates is over
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Federal Reserve Chairman Powell said on Thursday that the Federal Reserve is adjusting its overall policy-making framework to respond to significant changes in the outlook for inflation and interest rates after the 2020 coronavirus pandemic. “The economic environment has changed significantly since 2020, and our review will reflect our assessment of those changes,” Powell said.
Nick Timiraos, a "Fed mouthpiece" and Wall Street Journal reporter, pointed out that the Fed adopted the current framework five years ago and began reviewing it this year, and this review is unlikely to affect the way the Fed currently sets interest rates. Powell suggested that higher inflation-adjusted interest rates, or “real interest rates,” that emerged in the wake of the 2020 coronavirus pandemic may make some elements of the Fed’s current framework no longer appropriate. (Golden Ten)
